conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about your circumstances. Let me start by asking:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child - I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather some more details about your situation.

**Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, does Alex live with you, and are you the person who looks after him and pays for things like food, clothes, and other expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex doesn't actually live with me, but I do contribute to his upkeep. I help out financially with things he needs - you know, clothes, school things, that sort of thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. This is an important detail. Let me ask a follow-up question:

**Who does Alex live with, and does that person also contribute to his upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, and other expenses)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives with his ex-partner. Yes, she does contribute to his upkeep as well - she's the one looking after him day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to ask one more important question:

**Is only one of you currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or are neither of you claiming it yet?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Neither of us is claiming it yet - that's why I'm checking to see if I'm eligible.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Let me read the official guidance to give you an accurate assessment of your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Based on the official guidance I've reviewed and the information you've provided, I need to ask one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are your financial contributions to Alex at least equal to the current Child Benefit amount (which is approximately £25 per week for the eldest/only child), or are they less than this?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute £30 a week towards his upkeep, so it's more than that amount.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official UK government guidance and the circumstances you've described, here is my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's a summary of your situation:

✅ **You meet the eligibility criteria** because:
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- You're contributing £30 per week towards his upkeep, which is more than the Child Benefit amount
- You qualify as being "responsible for a child" even though he doesn't live with you, because you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after him

**Important things to be aware of:**

1. **Only one person can claim** - Since neither you nor Alex's mother is currently claiming, you can proceed with your application. However, it's worth discussing with her who will claim, as only one of you can receive it.

2. **If you both want to claim** - You would need to agree between yourselves. If you cannot agree, HMRC will decide who gets it.

3. **High Income Tax Charge** - Be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay a tax charge.

You can proceed with making your Child Benefit claim if you wish to do so!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). Although Alex does not live with you, you are contributing £30 per week towards his upkeep, which is more than the Child Benefit amount (approximately £25 per week for an eldest/only child). According to the official guidance, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after the child (for example on food, clothes or pocket money). Since neither you nor Alex's mother is currently claiming Child Benefit, you are eligible to claim. However, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, so if you both want to claim, you would need to agree between yourselves who will claim it. If you cannot agree, HMRC will decide.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Although Alex lives with his ex-partner, the claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit amount. This meets the eligibility criteria for someone responsible for a child who doesn't live with them. Since neither parent is currently claiming, the claimant can proceed with a claim, but should be aware that only one person can claim for the same child, so they may need to agree with the other parent about who will claim.

Since neither you nor Alex's mother is currently claiming Child Benefit, you are eligible to claim. However, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child, so if you both want to claim, you would need to agree between yourselves who will claim it. If you cannot agree, HMRC will decide.

Although Alex lives with his ex-partner, the claimant contributes £30 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit amount. This meets the eligibility criteria for someone responsible for a child who doesn't live with them. Since neither parent is currently claiming, the claimant can proceed with a claim, but should be aware that only one person can claim for the same child, so they may need to agree with the other parent about who will claim.
